kids.question: Who wrote this letter to Timothy?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:1 — "Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God."

kids.question: What did Paul say God has NOT given us?
kids.option_d: The spirit of a sound mind
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:7 — "For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind."

kids.question: What family member of Timothy had sincere faith?
kids.option_a: His grandmother Lois and mother Eunice
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:5 — "The unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice."

teens.question: What three things did God give believers instead of fear according to verse 7?
teens.option_a: Power, love, and a sound mind
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:7 — "For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind."

teens.question: What does Paul say God called us with according to verse 9?
teens.option_a: A holy calling, not according to our works
teens.option_b: A calling based on our good deeds
teens.option_c: A calling for the Jewish people only
teens.option_d: A calling that can be earned by study
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:9 — "Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works."

teens.question: What did Paul urge Timothy to stir up in verse 6?
teens.option_a: The gift of God which is in thee
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:6 — "Stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands."

teens.question: What has Christ abolished according to verse 10?
teens.option_a: Death, and brought life and immortality to light
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:10 — "Who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel."

teens.question: Who does Paul say turned away from him in Asia?
teens.option_a: All they which are in Asia, including Phygellus and Hermogenes
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:15 — "All they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes."

adults.question: What does Paul say was given to believers "before the world began" in verse 9?
adults.option_a: Grace in Christ Jesus
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:9 — "According to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began."

adults.question: What does Paul say he is persuaded about in verse 12?
adults.option_a: That God is able to keep that which he committed unto him against that day
adults.option_b: That Timothy will come to him soon
adults.option_d: That all of Asia will repent
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:12 — "For I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day."

adults.question: What does Paul tell Timothy to hold fast in verse 13?
adults.option_a: The form of sound words which thou hast heard of me
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:13 — "Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus."

adults.question: What does Paul say to guard by the Holy Ghost in verse 14?
adults.option_a: That good thing which was committed unto thee
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:14 — "That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us."

adults.question: Who does Paul commend for not being ashamed of his chain?
adults.option_a: Onesiphorus
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:16 — "The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain."

adults.question: What does Paul say Onesiphorus did when he was in Rome?
adults.option_a: He sought Paul out very diligently and found him
adults.option_b: He avoided Paul out of fear
adults.option_c: He reported Paul to the authorities
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:17 — "But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me."

adults.question: How does Paul describe his relationship to Timothy in verse 2?
adults.option_a: My dearly beloved son
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:2 — "To Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace."

adults.question: What does the phrase "the laying on of my hands" in verse 6 refer to?
adults.option_a: Paul's ordination of Timothy for ministry
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:6 — "The gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands." This refers to Timothy's ordination and commissioning for ministry.

kids.question: What does Paul tell Timothy to be strong in?
kids.option_a: The grace that is in Christ Jesus
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:1 — "Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus."

kids.question: What three examples does Paul use to describe a Christian?
kids.option_a: A soldier, an athlete, and a farmer
kids.option_b: A king, a priest, and a prophet
kids.option_c: A lion, an eagle, and a lamb
kids.option_d: A teacher, a doctor, and a builder
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:3-6 — Paul uses the images of a soldier (v.3-4), an athlete (v.5), and a husbandman/farmer (v.6).

kids.question: What does Paul say we should avoid?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:23 — "But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes."

teens.question: What does Paul say a good soldier does NOT do?
teens.option_a: Entangle himself with the affairs of this life
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:4 — "No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier."

teens.question: What must happen to an athlete to receive a crown according to verse 5?
teens.option_a: He must strive lawfully (compete by the rules)
teens.option_b: He must win every single race
teens.option_d: He must train for ten years
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:5 — "If a man also strive for masteries, yet is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully."

teens.question: What is the faithful saying in verses 11-12?
teens.option_a: If we be dead with him, we shall also live with him; if we suffer, we shall also reign with him
teens.option_b: If we pray enough, we will be rich
teens.option_c: If we study hard, we will be wise
teens.option_d: If we fast often, we will be holy
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:11-12 — "If we be dead with him, we shall also live with him: If we suffer, we shall also reign with him."

teens.question: What does Paul say about God's foundation in verse 19?
teens.option_a: It standeth sure, having this seal: The Lord knoweth them that are his
teens.option_b: It was laid in Jerusalem only
teens.option_c: It can be shaken by false teachers
teens.option_d: It needs to be rebuilt each generation
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:19 — "The foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his."

teens.question: What should Timothy flee from according to verse 22?
teens.option_a: Youthful lusts
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:22 — "Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace."

adults.question: What principle of faithful ministry does Paul establish in verse 2?
adults.option_a: Commit the teaching to faithful men who shall be able to teach others also
adults.option_b: Keep the teaching secret among apostles
adults.option_c: Write everything down for future generations
adults.option_d: Only share with those who pay for instruction
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:2 — "The things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the word of God in verse 9?
adults.option_a: The word of God is not bound
adults.option_b: The word of God is limited to Israel
adults.option_c: The word of God needs human defense
adults.option_d: The word of God changes with culture
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:9 — "Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound."

adults.question: What happens if we deny Christ according to verse 12?
adults.option_a: He also will deny us
adults.option_d: He will give us more time
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:12 — "If we deny him, he also will deny us."

adults.question: What does Paul say about God's faithfulness even when we are faithless (v. 13)?
adults.option_a: He abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:13 — "If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself."

adults.question: What does Paul say the workman approved unto God should do?
adults.option_a: Rightly divide the word of truth
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:15 — "Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth."

adults.question: What two men does Paul name as having erred concerning the truth?
adults.option_a: Hymenaeus and Philetus
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:17-18 — "Of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus; Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already."

adults.question: What false teaching did Hymenaeus and Philetus spread?
adults.option_a: That the resurrection is past already
adults.option_b: That Jesus never rose from the dead
adults.option_d: That the law should be abolished
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:18 — "Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some."

adults.question: In the analogy of vessels in verse 20-21, what kind of vessel should a believer be?
adults.option_a: A vessel unto honour, sanctified and meet for the master's use
adults.option_c: A vessel that is never used
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 2:21 — "If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master's use."

kids.question: What did Paul say the last days would be like?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:1 — "This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come."

kids.question: What did Timothy know from childhood?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:15 — "From a child thou hast known the holy scriptures."

kids.question: What does Paul say all scripture is given by?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:16 — "All scripture is given by inspiration of God."

teens.question: What characteristics of people in the last days does Paul list first in verse 2?
teens.option_a: Lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:2 — "For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud."

teens.question: What does Paul say about people having a form of godliness?
teens.option_a: They deny the power thereof
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:5 — "Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away."

teens.question: Who does Paul compare false teachers to in verse 8?
teens.option_a: Jannes and Jambres who withstood Moses
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:8 — "Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth."

teens.question: What happened to Paul in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra?
teens.option_a: He suffered persecutions, but the Lord delivered him
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:11 — "Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me."

teens.question: What does Paul say will happen to all who live godly in Christ Jesus?
teens.option_a: They shall suffer persecution
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:12 — "Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution."

adults.question: How many negative characteristics of last-days people does Paul list in verses 2-4?
adults.option_a: Eighteen or nineteen distinct characteristics
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:2-4 lists: lovers of own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy, without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good, traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God.

adults.question: What does Paul say evil men and seducers will do in verse 13?
adults.option_a: Wax worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived
adults.option_b: Eventually repent and turn to God
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:13 — "But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived."

adults.question: What four purposes of Scripture does Paul list in verse 16?
adults.option_a: Doctrine, reproof, correction, instruction in righteousness
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:16 — "All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness."

adults.question: What is the ultimate goal of Scripture according to verse 17?
adults.option_a: That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works
adults.option_c: That the church may grow large
adults.option_d: That all nations may be converted
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:17 — "That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works."

adults.question: What does the phrase "given by inspiration of God" literally mean in Greek?
adults.option_a: God-breathed (theopneustos)
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:16 — "All scripture is given by inspiration of God." The Greek word theopneustos means "God-breathed," indicating divine origin.

adults.question: What does Paul say about the type of women deceived by false teachers in verse 6?
adults.option_a: Silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:6 — "For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the folly of false teachers in verse 9?
adults.option_a: They shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men
adults.option_b: They will succeed in deceiving the whole world
adults.option_d: They will be destroyed by angels
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:9 — "But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as theirs also was."

adults.question: What is the significance of Paul mentioning Timothy's knowledge of Scripture "from a child"?
adults.option_a: It demonstrates the importance of teaching Scripture to children from a young age and shows Timothy's Jewish upbringing
adults.option_b: It suggests Timothy was a child prodigy
adults.option_c: It implies only children can understand Scripture
adults.option_d: It means Timothy wrote Scripture as a child
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 3:15 — "And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus."

kids.question: What does Paul tell Timothy to preach?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:2 — "Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season."

kids.question: What does Paul say he has finished?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:7 — "I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course."

kids.question: What is laid up (waiting) for Paul?
kids.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:8 — "Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness."

teens.question: What three things does Paul say he has done in verse 7?
teens.option_a: Fought a good fight, finished his course, kept the faith
teens.option_b: Built many churches, healed many sick, raised the dead
teens.option_c: Written many letters, traveled many miles, made many converts
teens.option_d: Preached many sermons, baptized many converts, planted many churches
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:7 — "I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith."

teens.question: Why did Demas forsake Paul according to verse 10?
teens.option_a: Having loved this present world
teens.option_b: He was sick and needed rest
teens.option_c: He was called to another ministry
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:10 — "For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica."

teens.question: Who was the only companion still with Paul?
teens.option_a: Luke
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:11 — "Only Luke is with me."

teens.question: What does Paul say people will want instead of sound doctrine?
teens.option_a: Teachers who tell them what they want to hear (having itching ears)
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:3 — "For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears."

teens.question: Who does Paul say did him much evil?
teens.option_a: Alexander the coppersmith
teens.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:14 — "Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works."

adults.question: Before whom does Paul charge Timothy to preach the word in verse 1?
adults.option_a: Before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:1 — "I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom."

adults.question: What five imperatives does Paul give Timothy in verse 2?
adults.option_a: Preach, reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine
adults.option_b: Pray, fast, study, teach, and baptize
adults.option_c: Heal, prophesy, cast out, deliver, and raise
adults.option_d: Build, organize, lead, manage, and grow
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:2 — "Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine."

adults.question: What metaphor does Paul use for his approaching death in verse 6?
adults.option_a: Being poured out as a drink offering (ready to be offered)
adults.option_c: Being swept away by a flood
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:6 — "For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand." The word "offered" (spendomai) means poured out as a drink offering.

adults.question: Who will receive the crown of righteousness according to verse 8?
adults.option_a: All them that love his appearing
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:8 — "Not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing."

adults.question: What personal items does Paul ask Timothy to bring in verse 13?
adults.option_a: The cloke, the books, and especially the parchments
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:13 — "The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the books, but especially the parchments."

adults.question: What happened at Paul's first answer (defense) according to verse 16?
adults.option_a: No man stood with him, but all men forsook him
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:16 — "At my first answer no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their charge."

adults.question: Despite being forsaken, what does Paul say the Lord did in verse 17?
adults.option_a: The Lord stood with him and strengthened him so the message could be fully known
adults.option_b: The Lord sent angels to break him out
adults.option_c: The Lord struck his enemies blind
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:17 — "Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known."

adults.question: What does Paul say the Lord delivered him from in verse 17?
adults.option_a: The mouth of the lion
adults.explanation: 2 Timothy 4:17 — "And I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion." This likely refers to Nero or the danger of the Roman court.
